elements.content: This is the first paragraph of chapter one. It fills vertical space so the document spans multiple pages.
A second paragraph continues to build out the page. The footer on every page must read the correct total once layout is complete.
A third paragraph ensures we push content onto a second page. Total pages can only be known after simulation ends.
Chapter two begins on page two or three depending on content flow. The footer here must still show the same correct total.
More body text pushes content further down the page, ensuring we reach at least two distinct pages in the output.
Chapter three exists to ensure the document reaches a third page. Every footer must show the same total page count: the final number determined after simulation, not a placeholder.
